segment-text: Jackie Filgo & Jeff Filgo Based on the novel by Jeff Kinney
2ND STUDIO DRAFT (2nd Revision)

DISPOSAL OF THIS SCRIPT COPY DOES NOT ALTER ANY OF THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH ABOVE.
INT. GREG'S BEDROOM
BLACK SCREEN. Soft breathing. SUPER: "Monday" is scrawled across the screen in Greg's handwriting. Then BLINDING LIGHT.
Greg. GREG'S POV: we find RODRICK HEFFLEY, an insolent sixteenyear-old, in our face. Rodrick is dressed for school.
(half asleep) What?
Rodrick shakes Greg awake.
Get up, you're going to be late.
END POV to find GREG HEFFLEY in his twin bed. Greg is eleven or twelve, average height, and really thin. Rodrick is going to town with the shaking thing, basically bouncing him on his bed.
(fighting him off) Quit it, Rodrick! Why are you -- what am I late for?
Middle school, what do you think? Today's your first day. Hurry up or Mom's gonna lose it.
Rodrick exits. Greg sighs.
INT. HEFFLEY KITCHEN

Greg, now dressed and ready for school, eats cereal and reads the comics with bleary eyes. He turns the kitchen TV on to AN INFOMERCIAL FOR A JUICER. He changes the channel, it's an INFOMERCIAL FOR BUYING FORECLOSURES. He keeps changing channels -- it's all infomercials. Something's not right. DIARY OF A WIMPY KID
What are you doing?
Greg looks up. FRANK HEFFLEY, Greg's dad, is there, wearing a t-shirt and boxers. He mutes the TV.
Getting ready for my first day of
middle... Greg trails off as he looks out a window. IT'S DARK.
EXT. HEFFLEY HOUSE - NIGHT
From across the street we see the Greg's house in the dark. An owl perches on a tree limb in the foreground.
INT. HEFFLEY KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
On Greg and Frank. ...school.
It's four o'clock in the morning. School doesn't start until next week.
SUSAN HEFFLEY, Greg's mom, unhappily enters. She's an attractive, harried mom. Frank watches MANNY, their wideawake two-year-old son, scurry past her, grab the cereal box and start eating out of it.
(sadly) You woke Manny up. He never goes back to sleep. (to Susan) He woke Manny up.
Have you lost your mind?
It was Rodrick!
INT. RODRICK'S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
They all stand in the doorway and look at Rodrick, sound asleep BUT NOW IN HIS PAJAMAS, snoring softly in his bed. But he -Go to bed.
SUSAN

As they leave, Greg turns back to look at Rodrick, who is in the same position only now he's smiling. Tight On a closed book. The cover reads 'Diary'. Pull back to reveal GREG HEFFLEY is holding the book and directly addressing the camera. Behind him is a WHITE BACKGROUND. Greg is a grown-up trapped in a kid's body. He has a very matter of fact, casual manner about him -- a worldly, confident attitude. Even so, he remains a likeable boy because we know his bravura just masks normal insecurity. He's Bill Murray as a kid.

Okay, first of all, let me get something straight: this is a JOURNAL, not a diary. (beat) Right, I know what it says on the cover, but when mom went out to buy this thing I TOLD her not to get me a book that said "diary" on it. This just proves Mom doesn't know anything. If I walk into my first day of middle school carrying this book around, I might as well be wearing a sign that says "punch me"!
INSERT SHOT: The cartoon drawing FROM THE BOOK of THE BULLY DISCOVERING GREG'S DIARY. The drawing becomes animated: the Bully punches Greg, sending him and the "diary" flying. Wimp!
1/22/09 - 2nd Studio Draft
BACK ON GREG addressing the camera.

The only reason I agreed to write in this thing is because one day when I'm rich and famous I'll have better things to do than answer peoples' stupid questions all day long, and this book is gonna come in handy.
INSERT SHOT: The cartoon drawing FROM THE BOOK of Greg at the 10 press conference. The drawing becomes animated as reporters shout questions at Greg.
Gregory! Tell us about your childhood!
Were you always so smart and handsome?
Greg waves his journal at them.
Here's my journal. Now shoo, shoo.
BACK TO GREG addressing the camera.

I actually should have started keeping a diary -- I mean journal! -- years ago. Now there's gonna be this big gap from birth to middle school. (beat) Anyway, I think future generations will be glad I kept a record of this time in my life, but the truth is when they make a movie or a TV mini-series about me, they'll probably just skip over the middle school years and go right to the part where I'm famous. (beat) Seriously, who's gonna want to see a movie about a guy who's stuck in middle school with a bunch of halfgrown morons?
He tosses the book on a nearby side table. THE BOOK AND HOLD.
INT. GREG'S BEDROOOM - MORNING
Still tight on the book, PULL BACK TO REVEAL the diary is on Greg's bedside table, and he is asleep in the bed. SUPER: "September.
Tuesday."
Susan, holding Manny, pokes her head in.
Get up, get up, it's your first day of middle school!
Greg stirs and opens his eyes. Suspicious, he checks the window for daylight to make sure it's legit this time. It is.
INT. HEFFLEY KITCHEN - MORNING

Greg, Rodrick, Manny, and Susan eat breakfast. Rodrick is working on a pile of flyers for his band Loded Diaper. The flyers feature a picture of Rodrick and his bandmates holding instruments and trying their hardest not to look like suburban teenagers. Rodrick is adding devil horns to the "L" in Loded and chuckling.
Mom's always saying I need to gain weight. But if you had to eat with my family, you wouldn't have much of an appetite either.

Pan around the table -- Susan picks her teeth, Rodrick chews his nails. Settle on Manny, who eats his cereal while sitting on a small training potty. Frank passes through on his way out to work, looks at Manny.
The toilet at the table, that doesn't feel right.
I don't want him to think it's shameful.
It should be a little shameful.
He grabs a piece of toast and kisses Susan. See you.
He exits.
(to Greg) So, middle school. Nah.
You nervous?
(shrugs)
Rodrick, give Greg some tips about middle school so he knows what to expect.
(to Greg) It's like prison. Don't ever look anyone in the eye, but don't look down either -- always be looking away, like you're lost in thought.
He looks at Greg skeptically.
I'm wasting my time, you'll be dead or home-schooled by the end of the year. No way.
Not helpful, Rodrick.
You're right. I'm sure he'll do great. He'll probably be Most Popular in the yearbook.
Rodrick grabs his flyers, gets up to leave. turns around. He stops and
Oh, and one more thing...watch out for the cheese.
He exits. Susan and Greg look at each other.
Okay, get Manny cleaned up and I'll drive you to school.
Manny looks at Greg, then dumps his cereal in his potty.
He did that on purpose! cleaning it up.
I'm not
It's your fault he's still potty training.
INT. DOWNSTAIRS BATHROOM - DAY
Greg, bored, perches on the vanity and watches Manny sit on the toilet.
Don't look down, Manny. The Potty Monster doesn't like it when you look at him. Manny slowly looks down into the potty -GREG (CONT'D) (covers his mouth) Rrrhaaaaaarrrrrr!
MANNY SCREAMS, jumps off the potty and runs out. END FLASHBACK.
INT. HEFFLEY KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
Greg chuckles to himself at the memory. Susan looks at him sternly. Greg gets to work and unhappily dumps out the potty into the sink.
INT. SUSAN'S CAR - MORNING SUPER: "First Day of School."
Susan stops in front of Whitmore Middle School in her older Honda. Greg looks out the window at students streaming inside. We see Susan wants badly for Greg to do okay here.
Want me to walk you in? No thanks.
A beat; Greg just sits there.
Are you going to get out?
Yeah.
He doesn't move.
Do you want me to walk you in? No.
Greg hops out.
Susan watches him head toward the school.
EXT. WHITMORE MIDDLE SCHOOL - MORNING
It's quiet as Greg nears the large metal front door. The school looks idyllic and nonthreatening. Next to the door is a cartoon poster of a character named "Wacky Dawg" welcoming new students.
Everybody's always saying how middle school is such a big deal. But to be honest with you, I wasn't really sweating it all that much.
He opens the door and is BLASTED BY A WALL OF NOISE.
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
Greg stands in the hallway. All around him is mayhem; it's loud and packed with kids roughhousing and jostling and shrieking. Greg begins to walk down the hall.

Okay, so I admit middle school was way different than what I was expecting. I mean, you got kids like me who haven't hit their growth spurt yet mixed in with these gorillas who need to shave twice a day.

GREG'S POV: EXTREME CLOSE-UPS of one big kid's BUSHY UPPER LIP FACIAL HAIR; another big kid's FREAKISHLY HAIRY AND MUSCLED FOREARM; another big kid's shirt open at the neck to reveal A TUFT OF YET MORE HAIR.
And not just their faces.
A hulking boy with acne, PRESTON, catches Greg's eye. Hey, Greg.
GREG
Greg squints at him, puzzled; he has no idea who it is. ...fella.
GREG (V.O.) (CONT'D) Half of these kids were in my school last year, but I could hardly recognize them.

Side by side, we see before and after photos of several of Greg's schoolmates. The "befores" are normal, cute grade school students. The "afters" look like bizarre hormone experiments. The first photo is of Preston, in which his clear grade school complexion becomes riddled with acne. In the ones that follow, full shiny hair becomes lank and greasy; others are stuck in weird, awkward phases of their growth spurts. In the last "before and after", A SMALL, NORMAL-LOOKING KID HASN'T CHANGED AT ALL.
Okay, so there were still a few normal looking people like me, but trust me, we were in the minority.
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
Greg continues to move through the hall, three older guys approach him. The BIGGEST GUY steps in his path. You new? Yeah. Newbie!
BIGGEST GUY
He punches Greg hard in the arm, his buddies laugh.
INT. HOMEROOM - DAY
Greg rubs his arm at his homeroom desk as ROWLEY JEFFERSON enters behind a couple of other kids. Only his face is visible at this point.
(calls) Hey, Rowley. GREG (V.O.) (CONT'D) Rowley Jefferson is my best friend.

The two other kids part and we get our first good look at Rowley. The word for Rowley is "thick". His head, neck, torso, and legs sit atop one another like a stack of blocks. He has a heartbreakingly open, honest, sweet face. HE IS ALSO WEARING A BRIGHTLY COLORED SERAPE. Greg cringes when sees Rowley's nerdy outerwear.
But that's definitely subject to change.
Rowley sees Greg and becomes excited, waving frantically.
(way too loud) Greg! Greg! Greg!
As Rowley heads over to Greg, Greg notices some of the other kids snickering at Rowley.
What are you wearing?
We just got back from Guatemala. (with a flourish) It's my serape!
Greg slouches in his seat as Rowley spins to show it off.
EXT. SPORT COURT - DAY
Greg, Rowley and the rest of the P.E. class wear gym clothes. Alarmed, Greg watches the large MUSCLED KID next to him slam a ball from one hand to the other as COACH MALONE talks.
Today you're going to learn how to play dodgeball right. First off, no aiming for the head.
(raises his hand) What happens to us if we hit somebody in the head?
Nothing. Okay now, listen up, when I blow the whistle that means...
Coach Malone continues to talk as...

Don't ask me what genius came up with the idea of dodgeball. My parents spent a lot of money on my braces so I'm pretty sure they didn't want me to get my teeth rearranged by some moron chucking a rubber ball at ninety miles an hour.
Greg gets hit in the mouth with a dodgeball thrown by a MORONIC GOON. His teeth fly everywhere.
EXT. SPORT COURT - CONTINUOUS
Greg's hand shoots up.
Uh, I have a heart murmur. My doctor says I can't play dodgeball.
Okay, well...just go sit over there.
He points to the edge of the sport court. Greg go and raises his hand. Rowley watches
I have...asthma.
Do you have an inhaler?
Huh?
Where's your inhaler? Where?
(sighs) Go sit next to the other guy.
Rowley runs off and catches up to Greg. As they walk...
The people running this place are trying to kill us. Maybe this is how they're gonna fix overcrowding in schools.
I gotta get my hands on an inhaler. (points to dodgeball) 'Cause that's scary.
I'm not scared, I'm walking away to make a point. This is just barbaric.
Like the Roman gladiators.
Greg and Rowley notice that ANGIE STEADMAN, an intense girl dressed all in black, has fallen in next to them.
Gladiators were actually slaves that were forced to fight to the death just for people's entertainment. Really?
Greg shoots Rowley a look that says "don't engage".
I'm glad somebody around here finally sees the parallel. (then) I'm Angie. This is my second year, and I'm telling you, middle school is nothing but a bad joke.
Through the following, Greg gets increasingly annoyed by Rowley's attention to Angie.

And the crazy thing is, all the adults know it, but they keep it to themselves. I mean, you hear them talk all the time about how fun high school was and how cute they were in elementary school. But you never, ever hear them saying they wish they could be back in middle school. They never mention it at all.
(locked in) That's true.
Greg pulls Rowley away...
(to Angie) I think the girls are supposed to be over there. As they move off...
Rowley, you gotta stay away from the nutsos. We're projecting an image here, it's a very delicate time.

They continue toward an empty spot on the blacktop, looking over their shoulders at the dodgeball game. They watch CHIRAG GUPTA, a little Indian boy, get nailed by four balls at once. Greg turns away just in time to see AN OLD GROSS SLICE OF CHEESE right in front of them. Whoa.
Check it out.
Is that...cheese?
FOREBODING MUSIC STING. Greg squats down next to it. It's a slice of cheddar, delicut. He reaches out with his finger, and is about to touch it -Stop!
CHIRAG (O.S.)
Alarmed, Greg and Rowley turn to see Chirag run up, battlewounded and out of breath.
What, Chirag?
You almost got the Cheese Touch. The what?
Chirag takes a deep breath, and then begins speaking almost mystically...

A long time ago -- no one knows how long -- that cheese just appeared on the blacktop. No one ever threw it away, and so there it sat, growing more foul by the day. Then one day, a kid -- some say his name was Bernard -made the biggest mistake of his life. FLASHBACK TO:
EXT. SPORT COURT - DAY
BERNARD, a curious kid, squats over the cheese and innocently touches it with his finger. Another kid (KID #1) sees him do it and points. Bernard touched the cheese!
A crowd of kids runs over pointing and screaming as if they see a zombie.
From that day on, Bernard had the Cheese Touch.
1/22/09 - 2nd Studio Draft EXT. SPORT COURT - CONTINUOUS
Chirag continues his tale...

No one would go near Bernard because if they touched him, they would get the Cheese Touch. Bernard became an outcast. Until, that is, he was able to pass the Cheese Touch onto an unsuspecting foreign exchange student.
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - DAY

DIETER, a happy looking German foreign exchange student, talks to a group of kids. Bernard sneaks up to the edge of the circle and touches Dieter on the shoulder. The other kids recoil from a confused Dieter.
Was soll das?
One kid (KID #2) points at him in horror.
Cheese touch!
And so it went, from student to student, from year to year.
As Chirag speaks, we see a ROGUE'S GALLERY OF CHEESE TOUCH VICTIMS through the years. Most are boys, but some are unhappy looking girls.
EXT. SPORT COURT - CONTINUOUS
As Greg and Rowley listen to Chirag...
The reign of terror ended when Abe Hall got it five years ago. He moved to California and took the Cheese Touch with him.
1/22/09 - 2nd Studio Draft EXT. WHITMORE MIDDLE SCHOOL - DAY
A group of solemn kids watch a station wagon pull away. We see ABE, a haunted looking boy, peering out the back window, his hands pressing against the glass. END FLASHBACK.
EXT. SPORT COURT - DAY
Chirag looks at them dolefully.
And so today, the cheese sits, patiently waiting for a new victim.
(blown away)
This is a terrible place.
Greg and Rowley look at the cheese. When they look up, CHIRAG IS GONE. Coach Malone blows his whistle, signalling the end of P.E.
Maybe we should just throw it away.
Rowley starts to reach for the cheese -- Greg quickly holds him back.
Geez, Rowley!
INT. CAFETERIA - DAY
The cafeteria is just as loud and untamed as the rest of the school -- there's yelling, pushing, maybe some food flying in places. Greg and Rowley move through the lunch line, pushing their trays.
By the way, second floor bathroom? No stall doors. I learned that the hard way.
What? If there's no doors people can see you. And all your business.
Yeah, well, worse than them seeing you is you seeing them. Trust me, you don't want to lock eyes with some strange guy sitting on a toilet. It's

a weird feeling. Rowley shudders. The lunch lady hands Greg a plate of yellow, lumpy goop (chicken a la king). He and Rowley head toward the table area. They see a few empty seats at a table of kids. to put his tray down. Greg starts
That seat's saved. For who?
It's saved.

Greg moves toward another empty seat at the table. That seat's saved. Greg moves to the last empty seat. He looks at the kid who just shakes his head. Greg looks around at the others at the table; they ignore him and Rowley as if they don't exist.
INT. CAFETERIA - MOMENTS LATER
Greg decides to try his luck with some shaggy looking skateboard kids. He crosses to their table, but they take one look at him and Rowley:
SKATE KID
INT. CAFETERIA - MOMENTS LATER
Greg and Rowley approach a table full of girls. Forget it. HAUGHTY GIRL Rowley turns to Greg.
So where are we supposed to eat?
1/22/09 - 2nd Studio Draft INT. CAFETERIA - MOMENTS LATER

Greg and Rowley have found a couple of lonely chairs in the corner and awkwardly try to eat with their lunch trays on their laps. FREGLEY, a thin hyper kid who breathes through his teeth in a way that often makes a whistling sound, approaches with a lunch tray.
Hey Greg Heffley. Want to see my secret freckle?
No thanks, Fregley.

Fregley sits down on the floor next to them. Greg tries to cut his chicken, but without a table it's hard to do and the chicken goes flying off of his plate and plops on the floor near Fregley. They all look at it.
Are you going to finish that?
EXT. WHITMORE MIDDLE SCHOOL - DAY
Kids stream out at the end of the school day. Rowley are mid-pack.
Okay, the first day wasn't great, but at least I got out with my dignity. Or so I thought.
(yelling to be heard) Hey Greg, you want to come over and plaayyy?!
An older kid, QUENTIN, overhears and steps in front of them.
What did you say?
(panicked) ...Flarb.
(to Greg) What'd he say?
I don't know. I don't even know that guy.
(calls to friends) Hey guys, check it out.
Quentin's two buddies, ARTHUR and JOE, approach.
This guy says to that guy, want to come over and plaayyy?
Greg grimaces at Quentin and his friends' scornful expressions.
This was hard to take, because in twenty years all these mouth-breathers would be working for me.
INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY - FANTASY

It's Greg, the same actor, but with a mustache and wearing a short sleeve dress shirt with a tie. He holds a clipboard and watches Quentin, Joe, and Arthur (also with mustaches) struggle mightily to put a heavy crate on a high shelf.
We finally got it up there, boss.
That's a shame. Because now I need you to take it back down, and after that I'm going to need you to put it back up there. But -Chop chop!
EXT. WHITMORE MIDDLE SCHOOL - MOMENTS LATER
A WHOLE CROWD OF KIDS are now gathered around Greg and Rowley as Quentin finishes gleefully repeating the story for newcomers.
(mocking) ...come over and...plaayyy!
THEY ALL LAUGH. Greg grabs Rowley and quickly pulls him away from the crowd to a space by the bike rack.
Thanks a lot, Rowley. What?
What'd I do?
We're in middle school now. Nobody plays anymore, they hang out.
What's the difference?
The difference is we can't act like kids anymore. Oh.
Okay, got it.
ROWLEY NONCHALANTLY GRABS HIS BIG WHEEL which is parked next to a bunch of bikes. Greg looks at the Big Wheel in horror. Rowley.
A Big Wheel?
(shrugs) I like being low to the ground.
Rowley takes off on his Big Wheel. Greg follows, conscious of the stares Rowley is attracting.
EXT. HEFFLEY HOUSE - DAY Rodrick's van with the name of his band "Loded Diper"
scrawled on the side is parked in the driveway. Next to the van is parked Rowley's Big Wheel.
INT. HEFFLEY LIVING ROOM - DAY

Greg and Rowley sit on the floor and play a racing video game. Manny sits on his tiny potty next to them and watches. Rowley is engrossed in the game, but Greg is clearly distracted. DIARY OF A WIMPY KID

When we got home I tried to unwind, but I couldn't stop thinking about school. And I'm worried about Rowley. He'll never be able to figure out how to make it through that place. He lacks nuance. I mean, you can beat him at a video game just by naming your car something ridiculous.
On the TV, Greg's car passes Rowley's. ROBOT VIDEO GAME VOICE Bad Fart ahead!
(falls over laughing) Bwaahahaha!
In imitation, Manny laughs and falls off his potty.
Let's race again.
Greg switches off the game. Rowley looks at him questioningly. Manny mimics Rowley and also looks at Greg questioningly.
Rowley, you need to focus. day sucked. It did?
Our first
Greg looks at Rowley like, "Is anybody home?"
We're nobodies at school. I don't know about you, but I never envisioned myself as the guy who has to eat lunch off his lap. That is okay for Fregley but not me.
My mom told me to just be myself and people would like me.
That would be good advice if you were somebody else.
EXT. HEFFLEY FRONT PORCH - DAY
Greg opens the front door to Rowley, who consults a book ("How to Make Friends in New Places with Jokes").
Knock knock! Huh?
Excuse me?
ROWLEY GREG
Thermos be some way to tickle your funny bone! Say what?
END FLASHBACK.
INT. HEFFLEY LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
Greg gives Rowley a sideways look. Rowley gets an idea and looks excited. Manny looks excited, too.
I think I may still have that joke book I used when I met you. Maybe I should bring it to school?
Greg looks at Rowley sorrowfully.
That book is not your friend. is going to help us.
No book
A beat while Rowley and Greg think. Rowley screws up his face in concentration and stares at the ceiling. Manny imitates him. Greg gets an idea.
We need to get into Rodrick's room.
I'm not going in Rodrick's room.
Rowley shakes his head "no". So does Manny.
1/22/09 - 2nd Studio Draft INT. BASEMENT - DAY
LOUD TERRIBLE MUSIC. Greg and Rowley peek around the corner to see Rodrick play the drums as he rehearses with his crappy band. They wince at the noise.
INT. RODRICK'S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER

Rodrick's room is a mess. Posters indicate he's really into Nine Inch Nails, Tool, and Slipknot. Rowley watches the door nervously as Greg, on his hands and knees, tosses aside junk from underneath Rodrick's bed. A half-eaten candy bar; a ouija board; and A GLOSSY MAGAZINE ENTITLED "MOTO MAMAS", the cover of which features a very buxom model in a thong bikini draped promiscuously over a motorcycle. Rowley eyes widen as he looks at the magazine.
Wow. I didn't know Rodrick was into motorcycles.
Greg pulls out the WHITMORE MIDDLE SCHOOL YEARBOOK, holds it up.
Rodrick's middle school yearbook.
He quickly pages through, finds what he's looking for.
Right here, Class Favorites. The best in the class. These people are famous. And I guarantee you they always get an actual table at lunch.
As Greg looks through the Class Favorites, we go CLOSE ON THE PICTURES.

This was it. If you can get yourself voted onto the Class Favorites page, you're practically an immortal. Even if you don't live up to what you get picked for, it doesn't really matter because it's on permanent record. Settle on the photo of the "Most Popular" guy, BILL WATSON.
People still treat Bill Watson like he's something special even though he dropped out of high school and works at the Food Barn.
INT. FOOD BARN - DAY
Greg and Susan are at the grocery store checkout behind TWO HIGH SCHOOL GIRLS. An older-looking Bill Watson is the bagger. The girls eye Bill and giggle.
Will that be paper or plastic? HIGH SCHOOL GIRL #1 (dreamy) Whatever you think, Bill.
INT. RODRICK'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
Greg points Favorites from the book out to Rowley.
This is where I need to be. Most Popular, Best Dressed, Class Clown...they should just give that to me right now. I'm hilarious.
Rowley notices the picture of the "Cutest Couple".
Hey, maybe we should go for "Cutest Friends".
You're kidding, right?
THE DOOR FLIES OPEN. IT'S RODRICK.
What did I tell you I'd do to you if I caught you in my room again?
You said you'd kill him.
Good answer, Rowley. You can go.
Rowley sprints out.
I'm sorry, Greg!
Mom and Dad aren't here. It's just you and me. And I'm going to really, actually kill you.

Greg makes a break for the door. He feints left, then heads right, but Rodrick stays with him blocking the door. Greg jumps up on Rodrick's bed and grabs the edge of Rodrick's prized Nine Inch Nails poster and prepares to rip it.
RODRICK (CONT'D)
Greg starts to rip it; Rodrick lunges for him. around him and runs out the door. Greg jumps
INT. UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
With Rodrick right on his tail, Greg squeaks into his room. He shuts and locks the door behind him, leaving an enraged Rodrick in the hall.
INT. GREG'S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
Rodrick beats on the door.
You can't stay in there forever, Greg! I don't care how long it takes, I'm going to be right here, waiting to kill you!
Greg peeks under his door. sneakers. Greg sighs. FROM GREG'S POV we see Rodrick's
INT. GREG'S BEDROOM - LATER
Greg creeps to the door, peeks underneath it. FROM GREG'S POV we see Rodrick's sneakers are still there. BAM BAM BAM, Rodrick bangs on the door. Greg jumps back.
Rodrick, time out, I have to pee.
No time outs!
1/22/09 - 2nd Studio Draft INT. GREG'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

Night has fallen outside Greg's window. Greg peeks under his door; RODRICK'S SNEAKERS ARE STILL THERE. Greg hops from one foot to the other; he really has to pee. Desperate, he puts his hand on the doorknob. Takes it off. Puts it back on, takes a breath, flings open the door and assumes a fighting stance... But the hallway is empty. RODRICK'S EMPTY SNEAKERS. What? Greg looks down, revealing Greg speeds off to the bathroom.
INT. BATHROOM - MOMENTS LATER
Greg stands over the toilet and starts to go. Suddenly, Rodrick leaps out from behind the shower curtain.
Raaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhh!!!
Aaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhh!!!
Startled, Greg jumps and twists and WE HEAR THE SOUND OF PEE SPLASHING EVERYWHERE.
INT. BATHROOM - LATER
Greg scrubs the bathroom floor around the toilet. stands over him.
EXT. SPORT COURT - NIGHT/DAY
It's dark. Time speeds up, the sun rises, revealing the cheese. A little steam comes off it as the day begins.
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - DAY

Greg and Rowley examine the Activity Board that is posted on a cork wall. There's a heading for each activity (Key Club, Swim Team, etc.) with a sign-up sheet underneath. Angie walks up behind them and listens.
You don't like any of this stuff.
I know, but if I'm gonna be a Class Favorite I have to make a name for myself.
Why do you want to be a Class Favorite?
Startled, Greg turns around to her.
I didn't say that.
Yeah, you did.
No, I...what probably happened is somebody yelled it over there and the sound bounced off this wall behind me so it sounded like I said it.
I've seen that on Scooby Doo.
A beat. Angie looks at them, trying to decide if they're kidding. She forges ahead.
